1‚Üí# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üí**Version:** 3.0 (Merged & Generic)
     4‚Üí**Last Updated:** December 2025
     5‚Üí**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools - works with ANY project
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üí---
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí## Table of Contents
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí1. [Overview](#overview)
    12‚Üí2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
    13‚Üí3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
    14‚Üí4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
    15‚Üí5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
    16‚Üí6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
    17‚Üí7. [Customization](#customization)
    18‚Üí8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)
    19‚Üí9. [Project-Specific Configurations](#project-specific-configurations)
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üí---
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí## Overview
    24‚Üí
    25‚ÜíThis automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md workflow rules by:
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí- **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
    28‚Üí- **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
    29‚Üí- **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
    30‚Üí- **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí### Supported Project Types
    35‚Üí
    36‚ÜíThis guide supports multiple project configurations:
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí| Project Type | Tech Stack | Build Tools |
    39‚Üí|--------------|------------|-------------|
    40‚Üí| **Static Sites** | Pure HTML/CSS/JS | None (direct browser) |
    41‚Üí| **React/Vue/Angular** | TypeScript/JavaScript | npm/vite/webpack |
    42‚Üí| **Full-Stack** | Any framework | npm + backend tools |
    43‚Üí| **Automation** | Python/Node.js | pip/npm |
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí---
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí## Quick Start
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí### Before Starting ANY Task
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí1. **Sync with main branch:**
    52‚Üí   ```bash
    53‚Üí   # For main branch
    54‚Üí   git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí   # For master branch
    57‚Üí   git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
    58‚Üí   ```
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí2. **Check workflow checklist:**
    61‚Üí   ```bash
    62‚Üí   cat .claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md
    63‚Üí   ```
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí3. **Create task file from template:**
    66‚Üí   ```bash
    67‚Üí   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
    68‚Üí   ```
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí4. **Get user approval (Rule #3):**
    71‚Üí   Use `ExitPlanMode` tool or ask user before coding
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí### For Static Sites (HTML/CSS/JS)
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí```bash
    76‚Üí# Start local server
    77‚Üípython3 -m http.server 8000
    78‚Üí# Or: npx serve .
    79‚Üí# Visit: http://localhost:8000
    80‚Üí```
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí### For Node.js/React Projects
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí```bash
    85‚Üí# Install dependencies
    86‚Üínpm install
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí# Start development server
    89‚Üínpm run dev
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí# Run tests
    92‚Üínpm run test
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí# Type check (TypeScript)
    95‚Üínpm run type-check
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí# Build
    98‚Üínpm run build
    99‚Üí```
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí### For Automation Scripts
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí```bash
   104‚Üí# Test with dry run first
   105‚ÜíDRY_RUN=true python automation/[script].py

   112‚Üí## Automation Tools
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
   117‚Üí**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
   118‚Üí**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí**How it works:**
   121‚Üí1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
   122‚Üí2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
   123‚Üí3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
   124‚Üí4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)
   125‚Üí
   126‚Üí**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
   127‚Üí```
   128‚Üí‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ
   129‚Üí  COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
   130‚Üí‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ
   131‚Üí
   132‚ÜíCode files were changed, but no task file is staged.
   133‚Üí
   134‚ÜíChanged code files:
   135‚Üí  - src/components/Modal.tsx
   136‚Üí  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
   137‚Üí
   138‚ÜíRequired Action:
   139‚Üí  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
   140‚Üí  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
   141‚Üí  3. Retry commit: git commit
   142‚Üí```
   143‚Üí
   144‚Üí**Emergency Bypass:**
   145‚Üí```bash
   146‚Üígit commit --no-verify
   147‚Üí```
   148‚Üí**Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies

   390‚Üí## Troubleshooting
   391‚Üí
   392‚Üí### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running
   393‚Üí
   394‚Üí**Symptoms:**
   395‚Üí- Can commit code without task file
   396‚Üí- No error message appears
   397‚Üí
   398‚Üí**Causes & Fixes:**
   399‚Üí
   400‚Üí**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
   401‚Üí```bash
   402‚Üí# Fix: Make it executable
   403‚Üíchmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
   404‚Üí```
   405‚Üí
   406‚Üí**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
   407‚Üí```bash
   408‚Üí# Check if file exists
   409‚Üíls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   410‚Üí
   411‚Üí# If missing, recreate from template
   412‚Üí```
   413‚Üí
   414‚Üí**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
   415‚Üí```bash
   416‚Üí# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
   417‚Üígit commit  # Correct
   418‚Üígit commit --no-verify  # Bypasses hook
   419‚Üí```
   420‚Üí
   421‚Üí**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
   422‚Üí```bash
   423‚Üí# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe or PowerShell for hooks)
   424‚Üí# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
   425‚Üí# Note: On Windows, you may need to use Git Bash terminal
   426‚Üí```
   427‚Üí
   428‚Üí---
   429‚Üí
   430‚Üí### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits
   431‚Üí
   432‚Üí**Symptoms:**
   433‚Üí- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
   434‚Üí- False positive error
   435‚Üí
   436‚Üí**Causes & Fixes:**
   437‚Üí
   438‚Üí**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
   439‚Üí```bash
   440‚Üí# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
   441‚Üígit add tasks/my-feature.md  # Correct
   442‚Üígit add my-feature.md         # Wrong location
   443‚Üígit add docs/task.md          # Wrong location
   444‚Üí```
   445‚Üí
   446‚Üí**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
   447‚Üí```bash
   448‚Üí# Check what's staged
   449‚Üígit diff --cached --name-only
   450‚Üí
   451‚Üí# Ensure task file is in the list
   452‚Üí# If not, stage it:
   453‚Üígit add tasks/my-feature.md
   454‚Üí```
   455‚Üí
   456‚Üí**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
   457‚Üí```bash
   458‚Üí# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
   459‚Üígit add tasks/my-feature.md   # Correct
   460‚Üígit add tasks/my-feature.txt  # Wrong extension
   461‚Üí```
   462‚Üí
   463‚Üí---
   464‚Üí
   465‚Üí### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template
   466‚Üí
   467‚Üí**Symptoms:**
   468‚Üí- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
   469‚Üí- File not found error
   470‚Üí
   471‚Üí**Fixes:**
   472‚Üí```bash
   473‚Üí# Check if template exists
   474‚Üíls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md
   475‚Üí
   476‚Üí# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
   477‚Üíls -la .claude/
   478‚Üí
   479‚Üí# Ensure you're in repository root
   480‚Üípwd
   481‚Üí```
   482‚Üí
   483‚Üí---
   484‚Üí
   485‚Üí### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File
   486‚Üí
   487‚Üí**Symptoms:**
   488‚Üí- Code commits go through without task file
   489‚Üí- Hook seems to not detect code changes
   490‚Üí
   491‚Üí**Causes & Fixes:**
   492‚Üí
   493‚Üí**Cause 1:** Code not in monitored directories
   494‚Üí```bash
   495‚Üí# Hook checks specific directories (src/, lib/, components/, etc.)
   496‚Üí# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it
   497‚Üí
   498‚Üí# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
   499‚Üí# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
   500‚Üí# Find the grep pattern line and add more directories
   501‚Üí```
   502‚Üí
   503‚Üí**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
   504‚Üí```bash
   505‚Üí# Hook ignores .md files in src/
   506‚Üí# (to allow README updates without task file)
   507‚Üí# This is intentional behavior
   508‚Üí```
   509‚Üí
   510‚Üí---
   511‚Üí
   512‚Üí### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying
   513‚Üí
   514‚Üí**Symptoms:**
   515‚Üí- Commit blocked but no error message shown
   516‚Üí- Silent failure
   517‚Üí
   518‚Üí**Fixes:**
   519‚Üí```bash
   520‚Üí# Test hook manually
   521‚Üíbash .git/hooks/pre-commit
   522‚Üí
   523‚Üí# Check for syntax errors
   524‚Üíbash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit
   525‚Üí
   526‚Üí# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
   527‚Üí# Some terminals don't support color codes
   528‚Üí```
   529‚Üí

   532‚Üí## Customization
   533‚Üí
   534‚Üí### Adding More File Patterns to Hook
   535‚Üí
   536‚Üí**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
   537‚Üí
   538‚Üí```bash
   539‚Üí# Current pattern (example)
   540‚ÜíCODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')
   541‚Üí
   542‚Üí# Add more directories
   543‚ÜíCODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/|components/|services/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
   544‚Üí```
   545‚Üí
   546‚Üí### Excluding Specific Files from Hook
   547‚Üí
   548‚Üí**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
   549‚Üí
   550‚Üí```bash
   551‚Üí# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
   552‚ÜíCODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$|\.d\.ts$)')
   553‚Üí```

   619‚Üí## Best Practices
   620‚Üí
   621‚Üí### 1. Always Start with the Checklist
   622‚Üí
   623‚Üí```bash
   624‚Üícat .claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md
   625‚Üí# Or use custom command
   626‚Üí/start-task
   627‚Üí```
   628‚Üí
   629‚Üí### 2. Create Task File FIRST
   630‚Üí
   631‚Üí```bash
   632‚Üí# CORRECT ORDER
   633‚Üícp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
   634‚Üí# Fill out the task file
   635‚Üí# THEN start coding
   636‚Üí
   637‚Üí# WRONG ORDER
   638‚Üí# Start coding first
   639‚Üí# Create task file later (will forget details!)
   640‚Üí```
   641‚Üí
   642‚Üí### 3. Update Task File as You Work
   643‚Üí
   644‚Üí```markdown
   645‚Üí## Todo Checklist
   646‚Üí
   647‚Üí### Phase 1
   648‚Üí- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE
   649‚Üí- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
   650‚Üí- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for input)
   651‚Üí```
   652‚Üí
   653‚Üí### 4. Fill Review Section Immediately
   654‚Üí
   655‚ÜíWhen work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
   656‚Üí- Summary of changes
   657‚Üí- Files created/modified
   658‚Üí- Testing status
   659‚Üí- Issues encountered
   660‚Üí
   661‚ÜíDon't wait - you'll forget important details!
   662‚Üí
   663‚Üí### 5. Stage Task File with Code
   664‚Üí
   665‚Üí```bash
   666‚Üí# CORRECT - Stage both together
   667‚Üígit add src/components/Modal.tsx
   668‚Üígit add tasks/add-modal-system.md
   669‚Üígit commit -m "feat: Add modal system"
   670‚Üí
   671‚Üí# WRONG - Separate commits (hook will block)
   672‚Üígit add src/components/Modal.tsx
   673‚Üígit commit -m "feat: Add modal"
   674‚Üí```
   675‚Üí
   676‚Üí### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names
   677‚Üí
   678‚Üí```bash
   679‚Üí# GOOD - Descriptive, specific
   680‚Üítasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
   681‚Üítasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
   682‚Üítasks/refactor-state-stores.md
   683‚Üí
   684‚Üí# BAD - Vague, generic
   685‚Üítasks/todo.md
   686‚Üítasks/task1.md
   687‚Üítasks/stuff.md
   688‚Üí```
   689‚Üí
   690‚Üí### 7. One Task File Per Feature
   691‚Üí
   692‚ÜíDon't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.
   693‚Üí
   694‚Üí### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES
   695‚Üí
   696‚Üí```bash
   697‚Üí# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
   698‚Üígit commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
   699‚Üígit commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
   700‚Üígit commit --no-verify  # Critical security fix
   701‚Üí
   702‚Üí# NOT for:
   703‚Üígit commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
   704‚Üígit commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later"
   705‚Üí```
   706‚Üí
   707‚Üí### 9. Keep Documentation Updated
   708‚Üí
   709‚ÜíIf architecture changes, update docs:
   710‚Üí```bash
   711‚Üígit add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
   712‚Üígit add tasks/add-undo-system.md
   713‚Üígit add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md
   714‚Üígit commit -m "feat: Add undo system"
   715‚Üí```
   716‚Üí
   717‚Üí### 10. Screenshot Before UI Changes
   718‚Üí
   719‚ÜíFor ANY visual/UI change:
   720‚Üí1. Screenshot current state
   721‚Üí2. Make ONE change (not multiple at once)
   722‚Üí3. Screenshot after
   723‚Üí4. Compare to baseline
   724‚Üí5. Get user approval before committing
   725‚Üí
   726‚Üí### 11. Test Automation with Dry Run
   727‚Üí
   728‚Üí```bash
   729‚Üí# CORRECT
   730‚ÜíDRY_RUN=true python automation/script.py
   731‚Üí# Verify output looks good
   732‚Üí# THEN remove DRY_RUN for real execution
   733‚Üí
   734‚Üí# WRONG
   735‚Üí# Just run live and hope it works
   736‚Üí```
   737‚Üí
   738‚Üí### 12. Meaningful Commit Messages
   739‚Üí
   740‚Üí```bash
   741‚Üí# GOOD
   742‚Üígit commit -m "feat: Add YouTube social link
   743‚Üí
   744‚Üí- Added new link to social menu
   745‚Üí- Updated animation timing
   746‚Üí- Tested on all browsers
   747‚Üí
   748‚ÜíGenerated with Claude Code
   749‚ÜíCo-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>"
   750‚Üí
   751‚Üí# BAD
   752‚Üígit commit -m "update stuff"
   753‚Üí```

   757‚Üí## Project-Specific Configurations
   758‚Üí
   759‚Üí### Static Site Projects (HTML/CSS/JS)
   760‚Üí
   761‚Üí- No build tools required
   762‚Üí- Use `python3 -m http.server 8000` for local testing
   763‚Üí- Focus on browser compatibility testing
   764‚Üí- Consider: GIF optimization, lazy loading
   765‚Üí
   766‚Üí### React/Vue/Angular Projects
   767‚Üí
   768‚Üí- Run `npm run test` before commits
   769‚Üí- Run `npm run type-check` for TypeScript
   770‚Üí- Run `npm run lint` for code style
   771‚Üí- Run `npm run build` to verify bundle size
   772‚Üí- Test in light mode AND dark mode
   773‚Üí
   774‚Üí### Full-Stack Projects
   775‚Üí
   776‚Üí- Test both frontend and backend changes
   777‚Üí- Verify API contracts unchanged (or documented)
   778‚Üí- Check database migrations
   779‚Üí- Test authentication flows
   780‚Üí
   781‚Üí### Automation Projects
   782‚Üí
   783‚Üí- Always use DRY_RUN=true first
   784‚Üí- Verify API keys are properly stored
   785‚Üí- Test error handling
   786‚Üí- Document required environment variables
   787‚Üí

# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide

**Version:** 3.0 (Merged & Generic)
**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools - works with ANY project

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#overview)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
7. [Customization](#customization)
8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)
9. [Project-Specific Configurations](#project-specific-configurations)

---

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md workflow rules by:

- **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
- **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
- **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
- **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists

**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.

### Supported Project Types

This guide supports multiple project configurations:

| Project Type | Tech Stack | Build Tools |
|--------------|------------|-------------|
| **Static Sites** | Pure HTML/CSS/JS | None (direct browser) |
| **React/Vue/Angular** | TypeScript/JavaScript | npm/vite/webpack |
| **Full-Stack** | Any framework | npm + backend tools |
| **Automation** | Python/Node.js | pip/npm |

---

## Quick Start

### Before Starting ANY Task

1. **Sync with main branch:**
   ```bash
   # For main branch
   git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit

   # For master branch
   git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
   ```

2. **Check workflow checklist:**
   ```bash
   cat .claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md
   ```

3. **Create task file from template:**
   ```bash
   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   ```

4. **Get user approval (Rule #3):**
   Use `ExitPlanMode` tool or ask user before coding

### For Static Sites (HTML/CSS/JS)

```bash
# Start local server
python3 -m http.server 8000
# Or: npx serve .
# Visit: http://localhost:8000
```

### For Node.js/React Projects

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Start development server
npm run dev

# Run tests
npm run test

# Type check (TypeScript)
npm run type-check

# Build
npm run build
```

### For Automation Scripts

```bash
# Test with dry run first
DRY_RUN=true python automation/[script].py

# Verify output before running live
```

---

## Automation Tools

### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook

**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7

**How it works:**
1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)

**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
```
‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ
  COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ‚îÅ

Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.

Changed code files:
  - src/components/Modal.tsx
  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts

Required Action:
  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
  3. Retry commit: git commit
```

**Emergency Bypass:**
```bash
git commit --no-verify
```
**Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies

---

### 2. Workflow Checklist

**Location:** `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md`
**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
**Enforces:** All workflow rules

**When to use:**
- Before starting ANY task
- During implementation (reference as needed)
- Before marking work complete
- Before creating git commits

**Sections:**
- Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
- During Implementation
- When Complete
- Git Commit Checklist
- Quick Reference for all rules

**View checklist:**
```bash
cat .claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md
```

---

### 3. Task Template

**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7

**Sections included:**
- Overview and problem statement
- Files to read
- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
- Todo checklist with time estimates
- Design considerations
- Implementation notes
- Testing checklist
- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)

**Create new task:**
```bash
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Good naming examples:**
- `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
- `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
- `tasks/refactor-state-management.md`
- `tasks/optimize-image-loading.md`

**Bad naming examples:**
- `tasks/todo.md`
- `tasks/task1.md`
- `tasks/new.md`
- `tasks/stuff.md`

---

### 4. Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow

#### `/start-task` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
**Purpose:** Intelligent task router

**What it covers:**
1. Routes to appropriate workflow based on task type
2. Creates task files from templates
3. Checks existing todos
4. Initiates planning mode
5. Gets user approval before implementation

**Use when:**
- Starting a new task
- Unsure of the workflow
- Need a quick reference

#### `/run` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/run.md`
**Purpose:** Execute prompts with auto-archiving

---

## Setup Instructions

### File Structure (Generic)

```
[project-root]/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .git/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ hooks/
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .claude/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ CLAUDE.md                   (global instructions)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ README.md                   (folder overview)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ settings.json               (Claude settings)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ settings.local.json         (local overrides)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ docs/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ automation-guide.md     (this file)
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ workflow-checklist.md   (step-by-step checklist)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ templates/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ task-template.md        (task file template)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ commands/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ start-task.md           (custom commands)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ skills/
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ [skill-name]/           (custom skills)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ tasks/                           (task files go here)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ CLAUDE.md                        (main workflow guide)
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ README.md                        (project documentation)
```

### Initial Setup (One-Time)

**Verify setup:**
```bash
# Check git hook exists and is executable
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check automation files exist
ls -la .claude/

# Make hook executable (if needed)
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

### For New Developers/Contributors

1. **Clone repository**
2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules)
3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md`
5. **Test git hook:**
   ```bash
   # Make a small test change
   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx  # or index.js, etc.

   # Try to commit without staging task file
   git add src/main.tsx
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should be BLOCKED

   # Stage task file and retry
   git add tasks/some-task.md
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should succeed

   # Revert test
   git reset HEAD~1
   git checkout src/main.tsx
   ```

---


---

## Troubleshooting

### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running

**Symptoms:**
- Can commit code without task file
- No error message appears

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
```bash
# Fix: Make it executable
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
```bash
# Check if file exists
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# If missing, recreate from template
```

**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
```bash
# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
git commit  # Correct
git commit --no-verify  # Bypasses hook
```

**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
```bash
# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe or PowerShell for hooks)
# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
# Note: On Windows, you may need to use Git Bash terminal
```

---

### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits

**Symptoms:**
- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
- False positive error

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
```bash
# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
git add tasks/my-feature.md  # Correct
git add my-feature.md         # Wrong location
git add docs/task.md          # Wrong location
```

**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
```bash
# Check what's staged
git diff --cached --name-only

# Ensure task file is in the list
# If not, stage it:
git add tasks/my-feature.md
```

**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
```bash
# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
git add tasks/my-feature.md   # Correct
git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # Wrong extension
```

---

### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template

**Symptoms:**
- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
- File not found error

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Check if template exists
ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md

# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
ls -la .claude/

# Ensure you're in repository root
pwd
```

---

### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File

**Symptoms:**
- Code commits go through without task file
- Hook seems to not detect code changes

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Code not in monitored directories
```bash
# Hook checks specific directories (src/, lib/, components/, etc.)
# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it

# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
# Find the grep pattern line and add more directories
```

**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
```bash
# Hook ignores .md files in src/
# (to allow README updates without task file)
# This is intentional behavior
```

---

### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying

**Symptoms:**
- Commit blocked but no error message shown
- Silent failure

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Test hook manually
bash .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check for syntax errors
bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
# Some terminals don't support color codes
```

---

## Customization

### Adding More File Patterns to Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`

```bash
# Current pattern (example)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')

# Add more directories
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/|components/|services/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
```

### Excluding Specific Files from Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`

```bash
# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$|\.d\.ts$)')
```

### Customizing Task Template

**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

**Example additions:**
```markdown
## Security Checklist
- [ ] Input validation added
- [ ] XSS prevention verified
- [ ] SQL injection prevented
- [ ] CSRF protection verified

## Performance Checklist
- [ ] Bundle size impact acceptable
- [ ] No memory leaks
- [ ] Animations 60fps
- [ ] Lazy loading where appropriate

## Accessibility Checklist
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Screen reader compatible
- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
- [ ] Focus indicators visible

## Browser/Device Testing
- [ ] Chrome
- [ ] Firefox
- [ ] Safari
- [ ] Edge
- [ ] Mobile (375px)
- [ ] Tablet (768px)
- [ ] Desktop (1024px+)
```

### Adding New Custom Commands

**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`

Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`
```markdown
---
description: Review task before marking complete
---

# Review Task Checklist

Before marking task complete:

- [ ] Review section filled out
- [ ] All files documented
- [ ] Testing complete
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Bundle size checked
```

### Adding More Skills

1. Create folder: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/`
2. Create file: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/SKILL.md`
3. Follow existing skill format (YAML frontmatter + content)
4. Update `.claude/skills/README.md`

---

## Best Practices

### 1. Always Start with the Checklist

```bash
cat .claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md
# Or use custom command
/start-task
```

### 2. Create Task File FIRST

```bash
# CORRECT ORDER
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
# Fill out the task file
# THEN start coding

# WRONG ORDER
# Start coding first
# Create task file later (will forget details!)
```

### 3. Update Task File as You Work

```markdown
## Todo Checklist

### Phase 1
- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE
- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for input)
```

### 4. Fill Review Section Immediately

When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified
- Testing status
- Issues encountered

Don't wait - you'll forget important details!

### 5. Stage Task File with Code

```bash
# CORRECT - Stage both together
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"

# WRONG - Separate commits (hook will block)
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
```

### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names

```bash
# GOOD - Descriptive, specific
tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
tasks/refactor-state-stores.md

# BAD - Vague, generic
tasks/todo.md
tasks/task1.md
tasks/stuff.md
```

### 7. One Task File Per Feature

Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.

### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES

```bash
# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
git commit --no-verify  # Critical security fix

# NOT for:
git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later"
```

### 9. Keep Documentation Updated

If architecture changes, update docs:
```bash
git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md
git commit -m "feat: Add undo system"
```

### 10. Screenshot Before UI Changes

For ANY visual/UI change:
1. Screenshot current state
2. Make ONE change (not multiple at once)
3. Screenshot after
4. Compare to baseline
5. Get user approval before committing

### 11. Test Automation with Dry Run

```bash
# CORRECT
DRY_RUN=true python automation/script.py
# Verify output looks good
# THEN remove DRY_RUN for real execution

# WRONG
# Just run live and hope it works
```

### 12. Meaningful Commit Messages

```bash
# GOOD
git commit -m "feat: Add YouTube social link

- Added new link to social menu
- Updated animation timing
- Tested on all browsers

Generated with Claude Code
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>"

# BAD
git commit -m "update stuff"
```

---

## Project-Specific Configurations

### Static Site Projects (HTML/CSS/JS)

- No build tools required
- Use `python3 -m http.server 8000` for local testing
- Focus on browser compatibility testing
- Consider: GIF optimization, lazy loading

### React/Vue/Angular Projects

- Run `npm run test` before commits
- Run `npm run type-check` for TypeScript
- Run `npm run lint` for code style
- Run `npm run build` to verify bundle size
- Test in light mode AND dark mode

### Full-Stack Projects

- Test both frontend and backend changes
- Verify API contracts unchanged (or documented)
- Check database migrations
- Test authentication flows

### Automation Projects

- Always use DRY_RUN=true first
- Verify API keys are properly stored
- Test error handling
- Document required environment variables

---

## Integration with Claude Code

### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code

1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
2. **Use task template** for all planning
3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
4. **Fill review section** when done
5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged

### Behavioral Guidelines

**Before starting ANY task:**
- Read `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md`
- Create task file from template
- Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)

**During implementation:**
- Update todos as you complete them
- Document decisions in task file
- Follow simplicity principle

**When complete:**
- Fill out review section
- Update docs/ if architecture changed
- Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)

**On every commit:**
- Stage code files AND task file together
- Git hook verifies compliance
- Commit proceeds

### Using Skills

Available skills can be invoked explicitly:
```
Use [skill-name] to [action]
```

---

## Appendix

### Git Hook Exit Codes

- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)

### ANSI Color Codes Used

```bash
RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
```

---

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow rules
- `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md` - Step-by-step checklist
- `.claude/templates/task-template.md` - Task file template
- `.claude/skills/README.md` - Available skills
- `.claude/commands/` - Custom commands

---

**Version:** 3.0 (Merged & Generic)
**Last Updated:** December 2025

**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. Follow the workflow for smoother, more consistent development across ANY project.
